factors.summary: Key sensitivities include the duration and impact of COVID-19, government budget constraints, and competition in technology and cybersecurity.

evidence.text: The impact of such matters includes, but is not limited to, the possible reduction in demand for certain of our services and the delay or abandonment of ongoing or anticipated projects due to the financial condition of our clients and suppliers or to governmental budget constraints; the inability of our clients to meet their payment obligations in a timely manner or at all; potential issues and risks related to a significant portion of our employees working remotely; illness, travel restrictions and other workforce disruptions that could negatively affect our supply chain and our ability to timely and satisfactorily complete our clients’ projects; difficulties associated with hiring additional employees or replacing any furloughed employees; increased volatility in the capital markets that may affect our ability to access sources of liquidity on acceptable pricing or borrowing terms or at all; and the inability of governments in certain of the countries in which we operate to effectively mitigate the financial or other impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic on their economies and workforces and our operations therein.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple key sensitivities including COVID-19 duration, government budget constraints, and competition pose significant risks.

The pandemic continues to affect demand and operations, with some business units experiencing increased demand for certain services, but overall, the impacts are expected to remain adverse, albeit to a lesser degree than in the previous fiscal year.

evidence.text: The Company’s operations for the first fiscal quarter of 2021 were adversely impacted by COVID-19. While certain business units of both Critical Mission Solutions and People & Places Solutions have experienced, and may continue to experience, an increase in demand for certain of their services regarding new projects that may arise in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, it is still expected that COVID-19 is likely to continue to have an adverse impact on each of Critical Mission Solutions and People & Places Solutions continuing into fiscal 2021, although to a lesser degree than what was seen in fiscal 2020.

Although this business disruption is expected to be temporary, significant uncertainty exists concerning the magnitude, duration and impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic, including with regard to the effects on our customers and customer demand for our services.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks are negative due to ongoing COVID-19 impacts affecting demand and operations.
